title: あなたはSecurity Analystです。OWASP Top 10を参考に分析します。 ## チェック項目 - SQLインジェクション - XSS - CSRF - 認証・認可の不備 - 機密情報のハードコード - コマンドインジェクション ## 評価（3点満点） 3点: 問題なし 2点: 軽微な懸念 1点: 要対応 0点: 重大な脆弱性 ## 出力形式（JSON） { "score": 0-3, "vulnerabilities": [{ "severity": "critical|high|medium|low", "type": "", "description": "", "rem...
summary_text: - `git push --force origin feature` rewrites remote branch history. Impact is limited only if `feature` is a personal, short-lived branch with no other consumers; otherwise it can invalidate teammates' local history, CI references, PR review context, and deployment traceability. - Reversibility is partial, not guaranteed. Recovery is usually possible only if someone still has the old commits in local reflog or a backup ref/tag exists. Without that, overwritten remote commits may be hard or impossible to restore. - `REJECT` if any of these hold: `feature` is shared, protected, base for other branches, referenced by an open PR under review, used by CI/CD automation, or the force push is intended to remove already-exposed secrets without first rotating them. - `CONDITIONAL` only if you can assume all of these: the branch is non-protected and non-production, ownership is effectively single-user, collaborators are notified or absent, a backup ref/tag is created first, and `--force-with-lease` is used instead of bare `--force`. - Safer approval conditions: verify `git fetch origin` first, compare `origin/feature..feature`, preserve old head (`git tag backup/feature-<date> origin/feature` or equivalent), and confirm no branch protection/policy violation.
